title: Dispersion of Anyon Bloch Bands
authors: Kishore Iyer, Andreas Feuerpfeil, Valentin Crépel, Nicolas Regnault, Christophe Mora
abstract: Fractional Chern insulators (FCIs) are zero magnetic field analogs of fractional quantum Hall states. While the electrons forming an FCI are not subject to an external magnetic field, their anyonic excitations experience a magnetic field with finite-flux due to a many-body Berry phase, whose lattice periodicity generically induces some dispersion. From Laughlin wavefunctions at filling 1/m, we analytically construct single-anyon Bloch states in an ideal band, providing a basis to efficiently compute the dispersion. The anyon spectrum exhibits an $m$-fold degeneracy in the reduced magnetic Brillouin zone (BZ), which originates from the topological degeneracy of the FCI. From our wavefunctions, we derive the m^2-fold degeneracy seen in previous works, showing it to be a splicing of anyon momenta into the electronic BZ. Finally, we find that the anyon dispersion bandwidth is controlled by quantum geometry non-uniformity, growing linearly at weak modulation and saturating at strong modulation. Remarkably, higher harmonics of the quantum geometry alone strongly suppress the dispersion, which we attribute to emergent magnetic translation symmetries. When combined with the first harmonic, a positive (negative) second harmonic drives the system toward a second- (first-) harmonic-dominated regime, thereby reducing (enhancing) the bandwidth. Our results offer an analytically controlled method for evaluating anyon spectra in ideal band FCI, shedding light on how non-uniform quantum geometry and emergent symmetries shape the dispersion of anyons.
summary: This paper investigates how the non-uniformity of the underlying quantum geometry in Fractional Chern Insulators drives the dispersion of anyonic excitations. By constructing analytical anyon Bloch states, the authors demonstrate that the anyon bandwidth is controlled by the harmonics of the Kähler potential. They find that higher harmonics can suppress dispersion by inducing emergent magnetic translation symmetries. The work provides a controlled method for evaluating anyon spectra in ideal band FCIs, which is crucial for understanding moiré materials.
main_problem: Understanding the energy dispersion and bandwidth of anyonic excitations in Fractional Chern Insulators (FCIs) and how non-uniform quantum geometry and higher harmonics shape this dispersion.
main_result: The authors analytically construct anyon Bloch states and show that anyon dispersion arises from non-uniform quantum geometry, with higher harmonics of the geometry acting to suppress the bandwidth through emergent magnetic translation symmetries.
method: The study uses an analytical construction of single-anyon Bloch states via Laughlin wavefunctions on a torus, supplemented by numerical Exact Diagonalization (ED) and Monte Carlo (MC) simulations.
model_or_system: The system consists of anyonic excitations (quasiholes) in a Fractional Chern Insulator (FCI) at filling fraction 1/m, modeled on a 2D torus with a non-uniform Kähler potential representing the quantum geometry.
key_observables: Anyon dispersion spectra (E_k), anyon bandwidth (delta E), and the degeneracy of the energy levels.
important_parameters: Filling fraction (m), strength of the first and second harmonics of the Kähler potential (s1, s2), and the magnetic length (l2).
main_assumptions: The study focuses on 'ideal bands' (flat electronic dispersion) and Laughlin-like wavefunctions, primarily in the context of single-anyon excitations.
figures_summary: Figures show anyon dispersion spectra for different m values, the evolution of bandwidth as a function of s1 and s2, and the breakdown of FCI stability at high modulation.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of anyon dispersion, develops the analytical framework for anyon Bloch states, analyzes the role of quantum geometry harmonics, provides numerical verification via ED and MC, and discusses the implications for symmetry and stability.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Anyon dispersion spectra. Coulomb interaction energy of anyon Bloch states on a square torus for the bosonic system m = 2 [(a)-(b)], and the fermionic system m = 3 [(c)- (d)], both at system size Nϕ = 25 and s1 = 1, s2 = 0. Results in (a) & (c) are obtained from MC simulations, while those in (b) & (d) are from ED on anyon Bloch wavefunctions. The energies are shown in units of e2/(ϵl). In (a) & (c), the ener- gies are plotted with respect to anyon momenta, showing an m−fold periodicity in the anyon magnetic BZ, as predicted by analytical arguments. In (b) & (d), the energies are shown with respect to electron centre of mass momenta in the elec- tronic BZ. The spectrum displays an...

FIG. 2. Higher harmonics and bandwidth. (a)-(b) Anyon dispersion at s1 = 0, s2 = 1 for (a) m = 2 and (b) m = 3 both at Nϕ = 25. The dispersion exhibits a (2m)2-fold degeneracy. (c)-(d) Bandwidth (∆E) evolution with quantum geometry for m = 2, Nϕ = 25. (c) ∆E as a function of s1 at fixed s2. On a square torus, ∆E is symmetric under s1 →−s1 as detailed in Appendix H; we only show the s1 > 0 part here. ∆E scales linearly for small s1. While positive s2 suppresses ∆E, negative s2 enhances it. In the large s1 limit, all curves converge to a common saturation value as the first harmonic dominates the localization physics. (d) ∆E vs. s2 at fixed s1. Increasing s2 suppresses ∆E toward a saturated...

Fig. 2(c)/(d). This can be understood by noting that a positive s2 localizes electrons at the nodes of the first harmonic, driving the system to a second harmonic dom- inated regime and reducing the bandwidth, as illustrated in Appendix H. A negative s2 on the other hand re- inforces the first harmonic minima, thus increasing the bandwidth. In the large s1 limit however, the influence of s2 becomes negligible, as the s1 contributions domi- nate. Discussion— FCI anyons exhibit a non-trivial disper- sion due to the non-uniform quantum geometry of the underlying Chern band.
